content:
[TOOL_ERROR] Shell variable expansion failed: $B1 is unset.
Common causes and fixes:
  • Missing assignment — set the value earlier in the same bash() call as a single-quoted assignment, e.g. B1='hello'; wpp-cli send 123 "$B1". Only the forms VAR='value' and export VAR='value' (single-quoted, no embedded single quotes) are propagated across segments; VAR=$(…) and double-quoted RHS are NOT captured.
  • Literal text — if you actually want the four characters "$B1" sent, quote with single quotes: '$B1'.
  • Default fallback — bash parameter-expansion forms like ${B1:-default} require B1 to be set first; they are not a substitute for an assignment.

[exit: 1]
[USER_HINT] The command failed.
